questions.q: In Hindu tradition, what kind of figure is Agastya revered as?
questions.explanation: Agastya is honored as one of the great Vedic rishis, a seer of hymns and a teacher of dharma.

questions.q: According to the Puranas, the sage Agastya famously drank what entirely in his palm?
questions.explanation: In the Puranic account, Agastya sipped up the whole ocean in his palm so the gods could find the asuras hiding beneath the waters.

questions.q: The sage Agastya is described in the Puranas as having an unusual birth from which object?
questions.explanation: Agastya is said to have been born from a pot (kumbha), which is why he is also called Kumbhasambhava and Kumbhayoni.

questions.q: Which mountain range did the sage Agastya, according to the Puranas, command to bow down and stay lowered?
questions.explanation: Agastya asked the rising Vindhya mountains to bow until he returned from the south, and they have remained lowered ever since.

questions.q: Agastya is honored as the sage who carried Vedic culture to which region of the Indian subcontinent?
questions.explanation: Agastya is celebrated as the rishi who crossed the Vindhyas and spread Vedic learning into the south of India.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, the sage Agastya blesses Rama when Rama is living in which setting?
questions.explanation: During Rama's forest exile, Agastya welcomes him at his hermitage and offers blessings and counsel.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, what does the sage Agastya gift to Rama in the forest?
questions.explanation: Agastya presents Rama with divine weapons, including a celestial bow, arrows and sword, to aid him in the trials ahead.

questions.q: Agastya is revered as a mantra-drashta (seer) whose hymns are preserved in which Veda?
questions.explanation: Agastya is honored as a Vedic rishi to whom several hymns of the Rigveda are traditionally ascribed.

questions.q: The emblem most associated with the sage Agastya in his pot-born legend is which object?
questions.explanation: The pot or vessel (kumbha) of his birth is Agastya's signature emblem, reflected in his name Kumbhasambhava.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, what was the chief reason Rama sought out the hermitage of the sage Agastya?
questions.explanation: Rama visits Agastya during his exile to honor the sage and receive his blessings, weapons and advice.

questions.q: By what name, referring to his birth from a pot, is the sage Agastya also known?
questions.explanation: Agastya is called Kumbhasambhava, meaning 'pot-born', after his miraculous birth from a vessel.

questions.q: In the Puranas, why did the gods need the sage Agastya to drink up the ocean?
options: To create dry land for farming
To expose the asuras hiding in the waters
questions.explanation: The asuras called the Kalakeyas had hidden in the ocean, so Agastya drank it dry to expose them to the gods.

questions.q: According to tradition, who is the devoted and chaste wife of the sage Agastya?
questions.explanation: Agastya's wife is Lopamudra, herself revered as a learned woman and a composer of Vedic hymns.

questions.q: In the Rigveda, Agastya's wife Lopamudra is remembered as one of the few women credited with what?
questions.explanation: Lopamudra is one of the rare women rishis (brahmavadini) credited with composing hymns preserved in the Rigveda.

questions.q: In the Mahabharata, the sage Agastya halts the arrogant rise of the Vindhya mountains by asking them to do what?
questions.explanation: Agastya bid the Vindhyas bow so he could cross to the south, telling them to stay low until his return, which never came.

questions.q: Which bright star in the southern sky is traditionally identified with the sage Agastya?
questions.explanation: The star Canopus, prominent in southern skies, is identified with Agastya in Indian astronomical and Puranic tradition.

questions.q: In the Puranas, the sage Agastya is said to have digested which mighty asura who had been eaten at a feast?
questions.explanation: Agastya ate the asura Vatapi, who used to revive inside his victims, and digested him with the words 'Vatapi jirno bhava'.

questions.q: The asura Vatapi, slain by the sage Agastya, had a brother named what who tricked guests into eating Vatapi?
questions.explanation: Ilvala would serve his brother Vatapi as a meal and then call him back to life inside the guest, until Agastya digested Vatapi first.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, in which forested region of the south is the hermitage of the sage Agastya located?
questions.explanation: Agastya's ashram lies in the southern forest lands around Dandaka, where Rama meets him during his exile.

questions.q: Which celestial bow, made by a divine craftsman, does the sage Agastya give to Rama in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Agastya gives Rama the great bow of Vishnu, fashioned by the divine architect, along with other celestial arms.

questions.q: Agastya is revered in the Tamil tradition as a foundational figure associated with what?
questions.explanation: In Tamil tradition Agastya is honored as a sage linked to the Tamil language, its grammar and early Siddha learning.

questions.q: When the sage Agastya drank the ocean dry in the Puranas, what consequence followed for the waters?
options: The ocean was emptied and had to be refilled later
questions.explanation: After the asuras were destroyed, the ocean lay empty, and it was later refilled, in some accounts through the descent of the Ganga.

questions.q: According to one Puranic account, the sage Agastya and the sage Vasishtha were born together from the seed of which two deities?
questions.explanation: In a well-known myth, both Agastya and Vasishtha arose from the seed of Mitra and Varuna, which fell into a pot, giving Agastya his pot-birth.

questions.q: Before marrying the sage Agastya, Lopamudra was, in the Mahabharata account, fashioned and raised in whose royal household?
questions.explanation: Lopamudra was created by Agastya and given as a daughter to the king of Vidarbha, from whom he later sought her hand.

questions.q: In the Mahabharata, why did the sage Agastya insist on marrying and having a son before completing his austerities?
options: To free his ancestors hanging in a pit
questions.explanation: Agastya saw his ancestors hanging head-down in a pit because he had no offspring, so he resolved to marry Lopamudra and beget a son.

questions.q: What was the name of the son born to the sage Agastya and Lopamudra in the Puranic and epic tradition?
questions.explanation: Agastya and Lopamudra's son is named Dridhasyu, sometimes also called Idhmavaha, born after their long austerities.

questions.q: In the Ramayana's account of his hermitage, why was the southern region around Agastya's ashram made safe and habitable?
options: He had dammed a great river
He had built a fortress wall
He had subdued the rakshasas troubling the sages there
questions.explanation: Agastya had overcome the rakshasas who tormented the sages of the south, making the forest a refuge of peace.

questions.q: Which devotional hymn to Surya, recited by Rama before slaying Ravana, is in the Ramayana taught to him by the sage Agastya?
questions.explanation: Agastya teaches Rama the Aditya Hridaya, a hymn to the sun-god Surya, to strengthen him on the battlefield against Ravana.

questions.q: Agastya is traditionally regarded as a founding teacher of which system of medicine and alchemy in South India?
questions.explanation: Agastya is venerated as a foremost Siddhar, a founding figure of the Tamil Siddha medical and alchemical tradition.

questions.q: When the sage Agastya drained the ocean in the Puranas, who had appealed to him for help against the asuras hiding in the sea?
questions.explanation: Indra and the devas, harried by asuras hiding in the sea, appealed to Agastya, who then drank the ocean to expose their foes.
